seed_instruction: I'm an undergraduate student, and I want to ask a professor if I can do some research with them in molecular biology. Please help me write them an email.
org_constraint: The tone of the email should be formal and professional.
new_constraint: Address the recipient with an informal nickname or abbreviation.
instruction: I'm an undergraduate student, and I want to ask a professor if I can do some research with them in molecular biology. Please help me write them an email. The tone of the email should be formal and professional. Address the recipient with an informal nickname or abbreviation.
response:
Subject: Inquiry Regarding Molecular Biology Research Opportunities

Dear [Professor's Last Name],

I hope this email finds you well.

My name is [Your Full Name], and I am currently a [Year, e.g., junior] undergraduate student majoring in [Your Major] at [University Name]. I have been following your work on [mention a specific topic, paper, or project of theirs] with great interest, particularly your recent findings regarding [specific detail].

I am writing to respectfully inquire if you might be accepting new research assistants for the upcoming semester. I have a strong foundation in [mention relevant skills, e.g., PCR, cell culture, data analysis] and am eager to apply these skills to your lab's current projects. I am especially interested in contributing to your research on [specific area of interest].

I have attached my CV and transcript for your review. Would you be available for a brief meeting next week to discuss your current projects and potential opportunities? I am happy to work around your schedule.

Thank you very much for your time and consideration.

Best regards,

[Your Full Name]
[Your Student ID Number]
[Your Phone Number]
[Link to your LinkedIn profile or personal website, optional]
